en.extract:
The Makira white-eye (Zosterops rendovae), also known as the grey-throated white-eye, is a small passerine bird in the white-eye family, Zosteropidae. It is also known as Zosterops ugiensis because Z. rendovae has often been used for the Solomons white-eye (Z. kulambangrae).
It is endemic to the island of Makira in the Solomon Islands archipelago. The Guadalcanal white-eye (Z. oblitus) on Guadalcanal and Bougainville white-eye (Z. hamlini) on Bougainville were formerly considered subspecies, but are now considered distinct species. It inhabits primary forest, mainly at 900–2000 m above sea-level but small numbers occur at lower levels.
It is a small bird, 12–13 cm long. It is fairly dark green above with a brownish tail, a variable dark brown area between the eye and bill and a narrow white ring around the eye. The underparts are dark grey apart from the green chin, pale centre to the belly and yellow undertail-coverts. The bill is blackish with a pale base to the lower mandible.

The '''Makira white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops rendovae'''''), also known as the '''grey-throated white-eye''', is a small [[passerine]] bird in the white-eye [[Family (taxonomy)|family]], [[Zosteropidae]]. It is also known as ''Zosterops ugiensis'' because ''Z. rendovae'' has often been used for the [[Solomons white-eye]] (''Z. kulambangrae'').<ref name=IBC>{{cite web|author=Internet Bird Collection|url=http://ibc.lynxeds.com/species/grey-throated-white-eye-zosterops-rendovae|title=Grey-throated White-eye (Zosterops rendovae)|access-date=9 January 2010}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal|author=Mees, G. F.|year=1955|url=http://www.repository.naturalis.nl/document/150019|title=The name of the white-eye from Rendova Island (Solomon Islands)|journal=Zoologische Mededelingen|volume=33|issue=28|pages=99–100}}</ref>

It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the island of [[Makira]] in the [[Solomon Islands archipelago]]. The [[Guadalcanal white-eye]] (''Z. oblitus'') on [[Guadalcanal]] and [[Bougainville white-eye]] (''Z. hamlini'') on [[Bougainville Island|Bougainville]] were formerly considered subspecies, but are now considered distinct species.<ref name=IBC/><ref>{{Cite web|title=Species Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/species-updates/|access-date=2021-05-28|language=en-US}}</ref> It inhabits [[primary forest]], mainly at 900–2000 m above sea-level but small numbers occur at lower levels.<ref name=Doughty>{{cite book|author1=Doughty, Chris |author2=Nicolas Day |author3=Andrew Plant |year=1999|title=Birds of the Solomons, Vanuatu & New Caledonia|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London}}</ref>

It is a small bird, 12–13 cm long. It is fairly dark green above with a brownish tail, a variable dark brown area between the eye and bill and a narrow white ring around the eye. The underparts are dark grey apart from the green chin, pale centre to the belly and yellow undertail-coverts. The bill is blackish with a pale base to the lower mandible.<ref name=Doughty/>
